title: Low-Rank Factorized Transformer Layers
novelty_summary: This idea proposes to replace all standard `nn.Linear` layers within a Transformer model with low-rank factorized approximations to reduce parameter count and computational cost, potentially improving inference speed and memory efficiency without significant performance degradation.

implementation_steps.change: Modify the `query`, `key`, `value`, and `dense` linear layers to use low-rank factorization (e.g., two smaller linear layers with a non-linearity or direct low-rank decomposition).
implementation_steps.done_when: The `BertSelfAttention` module uses low-rank factorized linear layers for its projections.

implementation_steps.change: Replace the `dense` linear layer in `BertIntermediate` with a low-rank factorized equivalent.
Replace the `dense` linear layer in `BertOutput` with a low-rank factorized equivalent.

expected_metric_change: Expect a slight decrease in model accuracy (e.g., 1-3% drop in F1 score on GLUE tasks) but a significant reduction in parameter count and potentially faster inference times.
